MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-18-2013 IN 206, HEARING ON APPLICANT/PLAINTIFF ANTONIO AMADO VAZ' WAIVER OF ADDITIONAL COURT FEES AND COSTS. APPEARANCE BY APPLICANT. THE COURT GRANTED THE APPLICATION AS TO WAIVER OF JURY FEES & EXPENSES; COURT-APPOINTED INTERPRETER FEES FOR A WITNESS AND FEES FOR A PEACE OFFICER TO TESTIFY IN COURT. THE COURT DENIED THE APPLICATION AS TO WAIVER OF FEES FOR REPORTER'S DAILY FEES; COURT-APPOINTED EXPERTS; DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANTS, WITNESSES AND OTHER NECESSARY FEES AND COSTS; INVESTIGATORS FEES AND COSTS. ORDER SIGNED, AND ENDORSED-FILED COPY MAILED TO APPLICANT. JUDGE: CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC., RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY AND CLIFFORD GILMORE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 6, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST IMMEDIATELY DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF HIS OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION AND REPLY. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANTONIO AMADO VAZ, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
MATT MCFARLAND, ESQ., 415.771.9850, FOR DEFENDANTS TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY AND CLIFFORD GILMORE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC., RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY, CLIFFORD GILMORE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer to the first and second causes of action for failure to exhaust administrative remedies is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts showing that he has obtained a right to sue letter from the Department of Fair Housing and Employment for these claims. The demurrer to the first, second, and third causes of action as to the individual defendants Shaw, Tekkey, and Gilmore is sustained without leave to amend. Only employers are liable for these causes of action. The demurrer to the first cause of action for failure to state a claim is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to allege sufficient facts for a claim of race or national origin discrimination. Plaintiff is given leave to plead further facts that give rise to his claim that he was treated differently on account of race or national origin, and facts concerning acts of discrimination or retaliation on account of his lack of citizenship. The demurrer to the third cause of action for failure to state a claim is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim for wrongful discharge based on disability discrimination or retaliation. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. All of the conduct giving rise to the claim stems from the employment relationship and falls within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Worker's Compensation Appeals Board. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting unfair business practices. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting a present controversy. If Plaintiff files an amended complaint, it must be filed within 20 days after entry of this Order. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANTONIO AMADO VAZ, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
MATT MCFARLAND, ESQ., 415.771.9850, FOR DEFENDANTS TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY AND CLIFFORD GILMORE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC., RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY, CLIFFORD GILMORE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is Granted in part and denied in part. The motion to strike all references to malice, oppression, and fraud is denied. These claims are not subject to a heightened pleading standard. The motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is denied. The claim is sufficiently pled. The motion to strike all prayers for attorney's fees is granted without leave to amend. The Plaintiff is pro per and not a licensed attorney. The motion to strike the portions of the prayers that are not a demand for judgment is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 425.10 does not require otherwise. The motion to strike paragraph (c) is granted as to the statement "damages for unpaid wages, including regular and overtime wages" with leave to amend. The complaint has alleged no facts indicating unpaid wages. The motion to strike paragraph (f) of the prayer is granted with leave to amend. The complaint has alleged no facts indicating that plaintiff was denied access to any records. The motion to strike paragraph (g) of the prayer is granted with leave to amend in light of the Court's ruling on the related cause of action for declaratory relief. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting a present controversy. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
